_note: Machine-readable registry of all public Σ₀ claims. Every claim must list status, assumptions, code entry point, artifact, and invalidation condition. No claim may be reported as theorem-backed outside its stated assumption set.

claims.label: Lyapunov collapse prevention — symmetric/normal dynamics
claims.description: For symmetric dynamics A = Aᵀ, the anti-collapse operator provably prevents Σ₀ collapse (covariance contraction to zero) when the excitation magnitude exceeds the Riccati sink rate.
assumptions: excitation is applied along null modes of A
no external state reset between steps
claims.invalidation_condition: asymmetric A (off-diagonal coupling > 0) or zero diffusion gain

claims.label: Anti-collapse performance over forced-collapse regime sweep
claims.description: Anti-collapse operator reduces collapse events in a forced-collapse scenario sweep (documented in docs/SIGMA0-COLLAPSE-CERTIFICATE.md).
assumptions: forced-collapse regime as defined in the certificate
specific Dynamics and parameter range from the sweep
results do not transfer to arbitrary dynamics outside the sweep
claims.invalidation_condition: dynamics outside sweep range; different noise regime; non-symmetric A

claims.label: Collapse trigger and early-warning control design
claims.description: The proximity function and NIS-based surprise spook are heuristic designs. Trigger thresholds are calibrated by hand and validated only over the documented test scenarios.
assumptions: proximity function is a reasonable heuristic for collapse imminence
NIS threshold (chi² with dim dof) is appropriate for this diffusion process
heuristic thresholds may produce false positives in novel regimes
claims.invalidation_condition: novel dynamics regime; non-Gaussian diffusion; very high or very low dimensions

claims.label: observe_only=True does not mutate x_next or sigma_next
claims.description: When InterventionPolicy.observe_only=True, forward_step computes trigger proximity/NIS but never mutates x_next or sigma_next. Proved by test: same output as no-operator baseline under identical seed.
assumptions: policy object is not mutated between construction and forward_step call
no other code path mutates x_next (e.g. custom subclass overrides)

claims.label: General sufficiency theorem for anti-collapse (non-normal/asymmetric dynamics)
claims.description: A general sufficiency theorem for the anti-collapse operator under non-symmetric dynamics has not been proven. The symmetric/normal case (Theorem 1) is proven; extension to the general case remains an open theoretical frontier.

claims.label: CIO-SDE is NOT wired to Keystone Chat, PCSF routing, cloud egress, or user memory
claims.description: Architectural audit: CIO-SDE forward_step does not call any server route, LLM API, PCSF selector, or persistent-memory writer. It is a standalone PyTorch module used in research scripts only.
assumptions: no future integration has been added without updating this registry
server.js and stream-chat.js do not import from src/cio_sde
claims.invalidation_condition: any import of src/cio_sde in apps/lantern-garage/ or server-side code
